california_probate.title: RUFADAA Definitions
california_probate.summary: Defines key terms including 'digital asset', 'fiduciary', 'custodian', and 'user' for purposes of fiduciary access to digital assets.
california_probate.applicability: Provides definitional framework for all digital asset access requests.

california_probate.title: Applicability
california_probate.summary: RUFADAA applies to fiduciaries acting under wills, trusts, and powers of attorney, as well as personal representatives of decedents' estates.
california_probate.applicability: Establishes that estate representatives have standing to request digital asset access.

california_probate.title: User Direction for Disclosure
california_probate.summary: A user may use an online tool to direct the custodian to disclose or not disclose some or all of the user's digital assets.
california_probate.applicability: Relevant when determining whether the decedent provided specific direction regarding digital assets.

california_probate.title: Terms of Service
california_probate.summary: If the user has not provided direction, the terms-of-service agreement controls, unless overridden by court order.
california_probate.applicability: Establishes the hierarchy: user direction > TOS > court order for determining disclosure obligations.

california_probate.title: Personal Representative Access to Digital Assets
california_probate.summary: The personal representative of a decedent may request a custodian to disclose digital assets of the decedent, subject to specified conditions.
california_probate.applicability: Primary statutory authority for personal representative requests to digital asset custodians.

california_probate.title: Disclosure of Content of Electronic Communications
california_probate.summary: Custodians shall disclose content of electronic communications to personal representatives if specified conditions are met, including court order.
california_probate.applicability: Requires court order for content of electronic communications; applies to email, messages, etc.

california_probate.title: Disclosure of Other Digital Assets
california_probate.summary: Custodians shall disclose a catalogue of electronic communications and non-content digital assets to personal representatives upon proper request.
california_probate.applicability: Provides authority for non-content metadata, activity logs, and catalogue information without court order.

california_probate.title: Compliance and Immunity
california_probate.summary: A custodian may charge a reasonable administrative fee and is not liable for good-faith compliance with a court order.
california_probate.applicability: Provides custodian safe harbor for compliance with court-ordered disclosure.

california_probate.title: Applicability of Other Law
california_probate.summary: RUFADAA does not limit a fiduciary's authority under other law to access digital assets.
california_probate.applicability: Preserves other legal theories for digital asset access beyond RUFADAA.

federal.title: Stored Communications Act — Voluntary Disclosure Exceptions
federal.summary: Permits a service provider to voluntarily disclose contents and records to a governmental entity or lawful consent.
federal.applicability: Relevant to whether a court order satisfies SCA requirements for disclosure.

federal.title: Stored Communications Act — Required Disclosure
federal.summary: Establishes standards for government-compelled disclosure of stored communications and records.
federal.applicability: Sets federal floor for disclosure requirements that state orders must satisfy.
